assignment.title: Animated Score Reveal & Combo Streak UI

problemStatement: After a multiplayer math duel ends, Matiks needs an immersive post-game score reveal screen. You must build it with production-quality animations.

designPhilosophy.overall: Premium, high-energy gaming aesthetic. Think Clash Royale end-screen meets Apple design polish. Every element should feel intentional, weighted, and satisfying.

background.style: radial or linear gradient, dark-to-deeper-dark for depth
background.notes: Dark, immersive background that lets animated elements pop. Subtle animated gradient shift for a living feel.

scoreText.notes: White with a soft golden glow/shadow to feel premium and victorious

comboStreak.notes: Hot gradient from orange to magenta, with a warm ambient glow behind the badge

rankText.notes: Rank number in cyan/electric blue to contrast with the golden score

shareButton.notes: Rich purple gradient with white shimmer sweep

confetti.notes: Vibrant, multi-colored particles for maximum celebration energy

scoreNumber.fontFamily: System bold / SF Pro Display Heavy or equivalent
scoreNumber.notes: Massive, heavy-weight number that dominates the screen. Use tabular/monospaced numerals so digits don't shift width during count-up.

scoreLabel.notes: Small, spaced-out label above the score for context

comboText.notes: Bold and punchy, emoji should be slightly larger than text

rankNumber.notes: The '#3' portion should be large and emphasized

rankSubtext.notes: 'of 1,200' in lighter weight and muted color

layout.structure: Single full-screen view, vertically centered content stack

spacing.notes: Generous whitespace between elements. Don't crowd the screen - let each element breathe.

hierarchy: Score label ('YOUR SCORE') - top of content stack, subtle
Score number (2840) - hero element, largest visual weight
Combo streak badge - secondary emphasis, centered below score
Rank reveal - tertiary, below combo badge
Share button - pinned toward bottom of content area, full width with rounded corners
layout.safeAreas: Respect device safe areas (notch, home indicator). Use SafeAreaView.
layout.responsiveness: Content should scale gracefully across screen sizes. Use percentage-based spacing or flexbox, not fixed pixel positions.

scoreGlow.description: Soft golden glow behind the score number that pulses subtly when count-up completes
scoreGlow.implementation: Use multiple overlapping Text shadows or an absolutely positioned blurred view

backgroundParticles.description: Optional: subtle floating ambient particles or bokeh dots in the background for depth
backgroundParticles.notes: Very subtle, should not distract from main content

comboBadgeShadow.description: Warm colored shadow/glow behind the combo badge for a floating effect

shareButtonShadow.description: Elevated shadow on the share button for depth and tappability

scoreCountUp.description: During count-up, add a subtle scale pulse on each major milestone (every 500 or 1000 points)

comboNumberSlot.description: The combo number (7) could have a brief slot-machine style flip-in before settling
comboNumberSlot.style: vertical slide with slight blur during transition

rankHashSymbol.description: The '#' symbol in rank could have a subtle color shift or glow when it appears
rankHashSymbol.effect: brief golden flash that fades to cyan

shareButtonIdle.description: Beyond shimmer, the button should have a very subtle breathing scale (1.0 to 1.01) to feel alive

shareButton.notes: Full-width rounded rectangle, large enough for comfortable tap target (min 48px height)

bouncyEntry.notes: Energetic bounce for combo badge entry - noticeable overshoot

settleSpring.notes: Smooth settle for elements landing into place

scoreCountDuration.notes: Score counts fast initially then decelerates as it approaches final value

fadeInDuration.notes: Standard fade-in for rank and other appearing elements

slideUpDistance.notes: Rank slides up 60px - enough to feel dramatic without being jarring

shimmerSweepDuration.notes: Shimmer takes 1.5s to sweep across, then pauses 2s before repeating. Not too fast.

orchestrationTiming.description: Fine-tuned delays between elements for a cinematic reveal sequence

timeline.event: Screen fades in, background gradient appears
Combo streak badge bounces in (while score still counting)
Score counter finishes (2000ms count + 400ms start delay)
Rank slides up with fade (200ms after score completes)
Share button fades in and shimmer begins

animationPrinciples: Ease-out on entrances (fast start, gentle stop) - elements arrive with energy
Spring physics for anything that overshoots - feels organic, not robotic
Stagger everything - no two elements should animate at exactly the same time
Looping animations should be subtle - draw attention without being distracting
60fps minimum - never drop frames. All animations on UI thread via Reanimated worklets
Avoid linear easing everywhere - it feels mechanical and cheap
Match animation intensity to element importance - score gets the biggest, most dramatic animation

reduceMotion.description: Respect the user's 'Reduce Motion' system setting
reduceMotion.implementation: Use useReducedMotion() from Reanimated. When enabled, skip animations and show final state instantly.
reduceMotion.notes: This is a quality signal - shows awareness of accessibility best practices

haptics.scoreComplete: Expo Haptics - notificationAsync(Success) when score finishes counting
haptics.comboReveal: Expo Haptics - impactAsync(Medium) when combo badge bounces in
haptics.shareButtonPress: Expo Haptics - impactAsync(Light) on button press
haptics.notes: Haptics should complement animations, not replace them. iOS only - gracefully no-op on Android.

touchTargets.notes: Share button and any tappable elements must be at least 48x48px

screenReader.scoreAnnouncement: Announce final score once counting animation completes
screenReader.accessibilityLabels: All interactive elements need proper accessibilityLabel and accessibilityRole

requirements.title: Score Counter Animation
requirements.description: Animate a number from 0 to the player's final score with a ticking effect and slight overshoot.
details: Animate a number from 0 to the player's final score (e.g., 2840)
Numbers should appear to 'tick up' with slight overshoot

uiPolish.textShadow: Soft golden glow (textShadowColor: rgba(255,215,0,0.5), textShadowRadius: 20) behind the number
uiPolish.tabularNumerals: Use monospaced/tabular figures so digits don't shift horizontally during count-up
uiPolish.completionFlash: Brief white flash overlay (opacity 0 -> 0.15 -> 0) when counter reaches final value to punctuate the moment
uiPolish.scaleOnComplete: Subtle scale pulse (1.0 -> 1.05 -> 1.0) when the number lands on its final value

requirements.title: Combo Streak Badge
requirements.description: Display a combo streak badge with bounce entry animation and a looping flame pulse effect.
details: Show a badge displaying: fire emoji + '7 Combo Streak!'
Entry animation: scale from 0 -> 1.15 -> 1.0 with a bounce
The flame emoji should have a looping pulse (scale + opacity) while visible

uiPolish.ambientGlow: Warm colored shadow behind badge (rgba(255,107,53,0.4)) for floating depth effect
uiPolish.emojiScale: Fire emoji should be 1.2x the size of surrounding text for visual punch
uiPolish.pillShape: Fully rounded pill shape (borderRadius: height/2) with generous inner padding
uiPolish.textShadow: Subtle dark text shadow for legibility against gradient background

requirements.title: Rank Reveal
requirements.description: Reveal the player's rank with a slide-up and fade-in animation, staggered after the score counter completes.
details: Player rank (e.g., #3 of 1,200) slides up from below the fold with a fade
Stagger this 200ms after the score counter completes

uiPolish.rankHighlight: The rank number (#3) in bright cyan/electric blue (#00D4FF) to contrast with golden score above
uiPolish.totalPlayersMuted: 'of 1,200' in muted gray (rgba(255,255,255,0.5)) and lighter font weight to create visual hierarchy
uiPolish.dividerLine: Optional subtle horizontal line or decorative separator between combo badge and rank section
uiPolish.numberFormatting: Use locale-aware number formatting (1,200 not 1200) for polished feel

requirements.title: Share Button
requirements.description: A 'Share Result' CTA button with a shimmer/glint loop effect and press feedback animation.
details: A 'Share Result' CTA with a shimmer/glint effect that runs on loop
On press: brief scale-down -> scale-up haptic-like feedback

shimmerEffect.description: Continuous shimmer/glint effect running across the button surface

uiPolish.elevatedShadow: Deep colored shadow (rgba(108,99,255,0.4), blur 16, offset y:6) for raised button feel
uiPolish.fullWidth: Full width minus screen padding, centered. Minimum height 56px for premium tap target.
uiPolish.shimmerImplementation: Diagonal white streak (30deg angle) that sweeps left to right across the button surface using translateX animation on a clipped overlay
uiPolish.pressedState: Scale to 0.96 + slight shadow reduction to feel like the button physically depresses
uiPolish.iconOption: Optional share/arrow icon to the right of text for visual clarity

animationSequence.description: The overall orchestration timeline for the post-game score reveal screen.

order.notes: Badge enters with bounce while score is counting or shortly after
Slides up 200ms after the score counter finishes
Appears after rank reveal, shimmer loops continuously

bonusChallenge.title: Confetti Particle Burst
bonusChallenge.description: Add a confetti particle burst using @shopify/react-native-skia when the score finishes counting.

constraints.details: Must use useSharedValue, useAnimatedStyle, withSpring, withTiming, withSequence, withRepeat
No Lottie, no Skia (unless bonus challenge)
iOS support also required if developing on Mac
Keep all animations on the UI thread for performance

evaluationCriteria.criterion: Correct use of Reanimated 3 APIs (UI thread safety)
evaluationCriteria.description: Proper usage of shared values, animated styles, and ensuring animations run on the UI thread without bridging to JS thread.

evaluationCriteria.criterion: Animation feel - timing, easing, spring config
evaluationCriteria.description: Smooth, polished animations with appropriate timing curves, spring damping/stiffness values, and natural-feeling motion.
Clean component architecture, separation of concerns, meaningful naming, and well-organized animation logic.
Consistent animation behavior and visual appearance across both platforms.

evaluationCriteria.criterion: Bonus: Skia canvas particle burst on score reveal
evaluationCriteria.description: Implementation of the confetti particle system using @shopify/react-native-skia with randomized trajectory, rotation, and fade-out.

reanimated3KeyConcepts: useSharedValue - create animation values that live on UI thread
useAnimatedStyle - derive styles from shared values without re-rendering
withSpring - physics-based spring animations for natural bounce
withTiming - duration-based animations with easing curves
withSequence - chain multiple animations in order
withRepeat - loop animations continuously or for N iterations
withDelay - delay animation start for staggering effects
runOnJS - only if absolutely needed to bridge back to JS thread
performanceTips: Never use setState to drive animations
All animation logic should use worklets (UI thread)
Use useAnimatedStyle instead of inline animated props where possible
Minimize JS-UI thread communication during animations

qualityBar.description: The bar for visual quality is high. This screen is the emotional climax of a competitive game. It should feel like opening a loot box or completing a challenge in a AAA mobile game.
mustHaves: No visual jank - 60fps smooth on mid-range Android devices
No layout jumps - elements should animate in, not snap and then adjust
Precise alignment - everything centered, spaced evenly, pixel-perfect
Color harmony - dark background, vibrant accents, consistent palette
Visual hierarchy - score is king, everything else supports it
Finishing touches - glows, shadows, gradients. Flat UI won't cut it.
The screen should make a player want to screenshot and share their result
redFlags: Flat white background with unstyled text
Animations that feel robotic (linear easing, no spring physics)
Elements appearing all at once instead of staggered reveal
Tiny text or cramped layout with no breathing room
Shimmer that looks like a loading skeleton instead of a glint effect
Janky or stuttering animations (JS thread overload)
Ignoring safe areas (content behind notch or overlapping home indicator)
goldStandard: Watching the screen feels like a 3-4 second cinematic moment
Each animation flows naturally into the next with no awkward pauses
The confetti burst (bonus) creates a genuine 'wow' moment
Haptic feedback syncs perfectly with visual beats
Someone watching over your shoulder would say 'that looks really good'
The screen would look right at home in a polished App Store game
